texto: For now the world keeps turning and I keep breathing, in and out, in and out. I breathe in the life that is all around me, in this garden, in this city, in the fields beyond it, in the seas beyond them and the shores on the other side; life that reaches out towards the unreachable, unknowable space that is beyond all of us and the stars that burn there.
explicacao: Citação de Clare Furniss sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “For now the world keeps turning and I keep breathing, in and out, in and out. I breathe in the li…” articula vida, books, fiction em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como luto e fiction.

texto: Our memories are what make us who we are. Some are real. Some are made up. But they are the stories that tell us who we are. Without them we are nobody.
explicacao: Citação de Clare Furniss sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Our memories are what make us who we are. Some are real. Some are made up. But they are the stori…” articula reflexao, memories, stories em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: You shouldn't be wasting your time worrying about what's going to happen after you die. It's pointless. Think about what's happening now. In your life. That's what's important. So change the subject, will you?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Clare Furniss que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “You shouldn't be wasting your time worrying about what's going to happen after you die. It's poin…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, inspiracional, living, com foco em mudanca e inspiracao.

frase_original: You shouldn't be wasting your time worrying about what's going to happen after you die. It's pointless. Think about what's happening now. In your life. That's what's important. So change the subject, will you?

texto: They get quieter over the years. They still whisper to you sometimes, but the world gets louder. You can see it and hear it again. There's a gap in it, where they used to be. But you get used to the gap; so used to it that you can hardly see it. And then some days, out of nowhere, you're making the tea or banging out the washing or sitting on the bus and it's there again: that aching, empty space that will never be filled.
explicacao: Citação de Clare Furniss sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “They get quieter over the years. They still whisper to you sometimes, but the world gets louder. …” articula reflexao, grief, again em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como luto e reflexao.
